mapping.a_element: grand canonical thermodynamic potential minimized to determine phase behavior
mapping.b_element: scaling functions derived from non-perturbative Renormalization Group flow

mapping.a_element: Polyakov loop and chiral condensate as order parameters
mapping.b_element: order parameter and longitudinal susceptibility as scaling functions

mapping.a_element: temperature and quark chemical potential as control parameters
mapping.b_element: external symmetry-breaking field and temperature as control parameters

mapping.a_element: renormalization group arguments for N_f-dependence in Polyakov loop potential
mapping.b_element: non-perturbative Renormalization Group approach for scaling functions

mapping.a_element: phase transition (deconfinement and chiral) driven by minimization of thermodynamic potential
mapping.b_element: critical behavior in O(4) universality class driven by scaling functions

brief_justification: Both papers employ renormalization group methods to study phase transitions and critical behavior in field-theoretical models. Paper A minimizes a thermodynamic potential to derive phase structure, while Paper B uses Renormalization Group flow to calculate scaling functions, but both rely on the same underlying machinery of order parameters, control parameters, and renormalization group techniques to analyze critical phenomena.
